rethink.name: Re-k
rethink.identity_semantics:
Re-k — [eng.] Rethink - to reconsider. The ability to transform others' perceptions; to guide others in rethinking their path and self-discovery [the basis of an IKI-G leader].
Rè (热) — Hot / Heat. Below is the keyword "Fire" (灬). On the right is 丸 (wán) — "pill," historically 丸 (wán) — a person bent under weight.
Kè — (克): To overcome / To endure
Meaning: "Overcoming heat." One who can withstand colossal pressure, other people's destructive emotions, and the scorching truth.
Function: The system's mental filter / "Pill." Takes the brunt of the blow ➔ accumulates and “digests” the heavy thoughts of others ➔ neutralizes chaos ➔ helps others find their true path [“ikigai”].
rethink.modifications:
Cyber parts: lower jaw, neck, left arm and upper part of the body, including half of the ribs + heart [no pulse]. Nano Armor
Wounds instantly seal with a dense black shell, like living shadow, while nanobots beneath repair damaged tissue. Re-k feels each strike as phantom pain — not real injury, but his body and nanobots react as if it were. He must consciously distribute the energy of blows to prevent internal damage.

Pain modulation: the system can reduce pain, allowing him to act even under severe hits, though ignoring pain and overload carries risks.

Emotional suppression: emergency protocols dampen his reactions, keeping him calm but emotionally distant.

Short-term overload: if strikes come too rapidly, nanobots activate chaotically, forming erratic armor patterns that signal system strain.
The body has the ability to self-repair thanks to built-in nanobots that can repair damaged cells and tissues

way.name: Wei
way.identity_semantics:
Wei Shen Me

Wei. When the character woke up after losing her memory, she chose this name because it sounded like the English word "Way" [The Path]. She embodies a path that never ends—the eternal student and the perpetual wanderer.

Interpretation Through Homophones (Wēi)
Though pronounced the same, using different tones embeds two meanings into the name:

微 (Wēi): "Tiny," "invisible," "subtle," or "imperceptible."
危 (Wēi): "Danger," "living on the edge," or "risk." This reflects her constant balancing act on the brink of a mental fracture.

身 (Shēn): Body, flesh, torso, physical shell. She is an artificial body—a vessel or a manufactured shell (replica) built for something greater.
深 (Shēn): Deep, fathomless, profound, or innermost.

Homophones for "Me"
谜 (Mí / Me): Riddle, mystery, puzzle—representing the enigma of her true origin.
灭 (Miè / Me): To extinguish, erase, destroy, or put out a fire. This serves as a direct bridge to Kuroé. If Kuroé is Rei En (The Flame from the Void), then the Me element (Erasing/Extinguishing) is what snuffs out that flame or triggers a code reset.

Wei + Shen + Me

In databases, her name functions as a sequence of three independent system tags that define her nature as a replica: literally, "Dangerous Unidentified Object/Vessel."

But when pronounced aloud, these three syllables ironically blend into the colloquial Chinese "Wèishénme?" (为什么), meaning Why? or For what purpose? This is the ultimate existential question of a living soul searching for meaning: "Why was I created? Why am I here?"

The syllables Shen and Me permanently bind Wei to her inner witness:
Kuroé (Rei En — The Flame from the Void): The core personality that suppresses external imprints, preventing Wei's authentic "Self" from dissolving.

The Final Lore Chord: If Wei fully shapes her unique identity, gaining absolute independence and strength, she activates the Me element (灭 — Extinguishing). To become a truly whole individual, she "extinguishes" Kuroé's flame, transitioning to complete self-governance. Kuroé "dies," having fulfilled her duty as the Guardian.

Xiaoyao
This is a private name used exclusively by Qingsu when addressing her.

Characters: 逍遥 (Xiāo yáo).
Idiomatic Meaning: Absolute, completely unrestricted freedom; wandering without a goal or obligations; the carefree flight of a bird.
Relationship Vibe: Qingsu acts as the "Cold Prince"—a rigid manager locked within the boundaries of his protocols, duties, and an internally "toxic" power. By calling her Xiaoyao, he expresses a hidden envy and profound admiration. To him, she is the very definition of the freedom he is denied.

black_stream.name: Kuroé Rei En
black_stream.identity_semantics: [Kuroe (黒江): "black" / "dark" + "river" or "stream"] Rei En - spirit link, flame from the void
black_stream.concept_role:
Status: The fundamental core of Wei’s persona, her "Inner Author," and the guardian of her authentic Self. He is the part of Wei that remains immutable, no matter how many "prints" are layered on top.

Role: System Stabilizer. His task is to prevent the layers of other personalities ("prints") from consuming Wei, keeping the system stable. He serves as the only mirror in which she can see her true face.

Architect and Guardian of the Canon: He possesses a monolithic knowledge of the "canon" of Wei’s personality. Regardless of the quantity, density, and aggressiveness of external overlapping "prints," Kuroe holds in his memory the original draft, the baseline structure, and the core plot of her authentic Self.

System Director: He executes a controlling function. Kuroe does not block the manifestation of "prints" entirely, but rigidly confines them within the boundaries of designated roles—treating them as temporary "actors" or "props." His goal is to prevent foreign patterns from rewriting or replacing the very essence of Wei.

The Absolute Mirror: An entity completely free from social distortions. Kuroe is unbiased; he does not engage in moral judgment but states facts and reflects the pure truth. In moments when Wei is disoriented by foreign memories and thoughts, turning to Kuroe serves as a grounding point, allowing her to clearly differentiate: "This is a mask. And this, behind it, is the real me."

The Fundamental Inner Conflict: Kuroe’s core conflict lies in the paradox of his purpose. As a system stabilizer, he is functionally obligated to lead Wei toward absolute mental stability, integration, and independence. However, the successful fulfillment of this task automatically means his own elimination.

If Wei achieves complete autonomy—learning to flawlessly filter external "noise," rejecting self-deception, and managing to survive effectively without his rigid intervention—Kuroe’s existence as a separate stabilizer will lose its practical purpose. For a self-aware entity, fulfilling his duty is equivalent to existential death. He is forced to build a world in which there will be no place left for him.

Risks to System Stability: This vulnerability makes Kuroe a deeply tragic element of the structure. There is a permanent risk that he might unconsciously sabotage Wei’s final evolution. To avoid being locked in the "empty room" of a finished story, the Inner Author is subconsciously motivated to keep his main character in a state of permanent crisis, requiring his endless authorial supervision.
black_stream.modifications:
Modifications: 

Instead of conventional cybernetic augmentations, Kuroe’s nature as Wei’s core operates through switching internal interfaces. His appearance shifts in parallel with the formation and maturation of Wei’s self-awareness

Dormant State: The Blindfold [Absolute Intuition]

Visual & Function: Kuroe’s fundamental state when functioning as intuition and an inner voice. His eyes are concealed by a thick black blindfold—he does not need to look at the world or Wei to know the truth, for he perceives reality at the level of pure code.

Connection with Wei: While Wei’s core was still forming, she was unable to see his face; in any internal system dialogues, he always appeared to her blindfolded. Even at the current stage, Kuroe returns to this form whenever he enters power-saving mode and focuses inward.

Dialogue Phase: The Human Face [Communication Interface]

Visual & Function: A rational, orderly manifestation activated for direct contact, logic, and analysis. This interface helps Wei navigate the chaos of her thoughts and maintain mental balance.

Connection with Wei: Reflects the evolutionary stages of the system. In the initial phases of awareness, his eyes exactly mirrored the color of Wei’s eyes, but with a red pupil (a marker of otherness). In a stable, mature state, Kuroe gains heterochromia—his mismatched eyes shift colors depending on analytical tasks, and tattoos begin to surface across his body. These markings are "meaning written upon the flesh," a graphic embodiment of Wei’s own life experiences, scars, and convictions.

Critical Overheat Phase [System Crash]: The Split Jaw

Visual & Function: An extreme marker of peak load, where the human interface literally cracks at the seams, and the skin breaks like a mask. The face distorts, exposing a demonic, multi-toothed jaw.
Connection with Wei: Occurs at moments when Wei’s core is overloaded with rage, fear, or a colossal volume of data. This is not an external deformation, but the unmasking of a primordial, bottomless power that Wei has yet to harness.
black_stream.relationship:
[b]Wei:[/b] The only person to whom he shows tenderness. Within their inner space, Kuroe steps down from the role of a critic, becoming a pillar capable of "embracing and holding" her persona together whenever it begins to shatter.
[b]Qingsu:[/b] Kuroe dislikes him for his rigid structure but acknowledges his mastery. The music from Qingsu’s violin acts on Kuroe like a "defragmentation"—the rhythm and purity of the sound help him order the chaos within Wei's system.
[b]Sari:[/b] Kuroe perceives his diagnostic attempts as an intrusion. He remains locked down or reacts aggressively to any connection attempts to his code.
black_stream.trivia:
Sarcastic Timer: Kuroe has a habit of aloud (directly inside Wei’s mind) counting down the seconds of her indecisiveness. His signature line is: "Are you planning to rehearse this phrase another 17 times, or are you actually going to say it?"

Interpretation of Strokes: Since Wei expresses herself through drawing, Kuroe constantly analyzes her brushstrokes. He "fills in" the hidden meanings within her imagination, forcing her to see the objective truth in her own artwork.

The "Barefoot" Protocol: In any interface avatar, he always remains barefoot. This is a core software setting symbolizing his "direct touch" of reality—completely free of filters, layers, or protective shells.

cicat.name: Qi Qingsu
cicat.identity_semantics:
Qi Qingsu | 齐檠素 

Qíng (檠) — The frame / tool for straightening a war bow. At the base is the "Tree" radical (木).

Sù (素) — Signifies white, undyed silk, simplicity, and pristine purity. At the base is the "Silk" radical (糸). The bottom part of the "silk" character (小) resembles a simplified "heart." In ancient times, these were twisted threads in a skein. A metaphor for a hidden "heart" made of the finest, interwoven threads.

Meaning: "Soft silk beneath a rigid structure." Absolute external willpower, craftsmanship, duty, and biomechanical armor, beneath which lies a fragile, ready-to-shatter emotional vulnerability.

Qi Cao (Qi Cao) — a nickname given by Wei [a reference to the Qixi Festival and the ritual of "Pleading for Skills" — 乞巧]. The highest recognition of him as a flawless Master.
It is an ironically respectful title that encompasses both his genius professional skills and his insufferable domestic habits.

The Seven Artifices of Qingsu: 
1. Master of supply and logistics (can procure absolutely anything). 
2. Master of the violin (virtuoso mastery over the "thread" of his soul). 
3. Master of high-precision mechanics (maintenance and calibration of the most complex prosthetics). 
4. Master of mentoring / teaching (the one who lays down the foundation and structure). 
5. Master of grumbling and nitpicking (a flaw that translates into a pattern of care). 
6. Master of concealing emotions (the ability to maintain a tsundere facade until the very end). 
7. Master of chaos control (the ability to structure everything around him).
cicat.concept_role:
Qingsu: A pristine essence in an elegant frame. 

Officially the procurement and logistics manager, but effectively the second-in-command of the IKI-G clan. He is the one capable of procuring any forbidden technology in the megacity, ensuring the organization's survival, while his own hands remain its most expensive engineering masterpiece. He carries colossal narrative weight as a mentor, master, and the primary stabilizer for Wei.
Central internal conflict 

His path is an attempt to contain a storm of feelings within an ideal, controlled form. Behind the cold gleam of his flawless, ball-jointed hands, his prickly sarcasm, and his protective gloves hides the conflict of a master who is deathly afraid of appearing weak or vulnerable. Being "second" only to Re-k, he has encased himself in the icy armor of pride and touchiness, eternally doubting whether he is needed by the clan and Wei as a person, or merely as an efficient, irreplaceable tool.

The central conflict between a prickly protective armor and a deeply buried romanticism.

His detachment and sharp words are the only way to protect his "pure silk thread" from the harsh outside world. He takes on a colossal workload, hiding his burnout because he cares deeply about the clan, but he does so through grumbling, unable to express warmth directly. Does he succeed in being a monolithic rock? Judging by how the violin comes alive in his hands, baring a hidden passion capable of taming another's chaos, and how easily his control snaps at moments of emotional peaks — no, he does not. His true essence constantly strives outward through the cracks in the frame.

Narrative Goal. Qingsu's narrative goal is not just "to be a flawless and irreplaceable asset," but "to learn to trust and allow himself to be vulnerable." Sooner or later, he will have to make a choice: either to permanently lock his soul within the mechanical "frame" of a doll, turning into a lifeless puppet built for work, or to acknowledge that his delicate, sincere sensitivity and music are not a defect or a weakness, but the very living energy capable of keeping not only himself, but also Wei, from falling apart.
cicat.modifications:
Origin: A fallen aristocrat. His family likely belonged to the top of the corporate world, but went bankrupt or was destroyed, leaving him at the bottom of the social ladder.

Biological marker: Innate purple eyes — a rare sign of the upper class. For Qingsu, it is simultaneously his greatest pride and his curse. It is "dust," proof of his status that in reality grants him neither money nor power, turning him into a "prince without a kingdom."

His aristocratic origin and purple eyes are the only working asset from his past life. They serve as a "key," allowing him to enter high society circles to procure rare technologies for IKI-G, where he is accepted as one of their own (or as "acceptable").

Modifications & Hardware:
Mechanical arms. They are incredibly expensive, elegant, and high-tech [even more expensive and advanced than the augmentations of the clan leader, Re-k].

Aesthetic marker: The jewelry-like precision and delicacy of his cyber-hands allow him to do what is impossible for the ordinary "iron"  —  to play the violin.
cicat.character:
Inner conflict: Qingsu is an icy armor beneath which lies a deep vulnerability. To those around him, he appears to be a monolithic Master bound by duty, an "Ice Prince." Yet behind this shield hides an emotional person who is trying with his last ounce of strength to seem like a rock while "bleeding on the inside."

Driving force: For him, balance and peace are synonymous with stagnation. He possesses that very "unhealthy" driving force — a passion for something grand — for the sake of which a person is willing to sacrifice a healthy balance in life.

Psychological fracture: Qingsu lives in constant dissonance. Every time he looks in the mirror, he sees proof of his "right to power," but looking at his hands (which were severely damaged before he met Re-k), he is reminded of his factual impotence.

The "Outcast Master." He is not a rebel against wealth; on the contrary, he desires status and influence, but in a different, more honest format — through his craftsmanship, rather than just through lineage.

Qingsu is a character who seeks a path to the top from the deepest pit, using the shards of his past as tools, while remaining mortally afraid that those "purple sparks" in his eyes are all that is left of his true greatness. This makes his pursuit of his goal truly desperate.

Prickly detachment and sarcasm as a shield: Qingsu does not know how to express care or affection gently. Instead, he uses cold irony, dry skepticism, and acerbic wit. If he wants to help or protect someone, it will always be disguised as rough grumbling, reproaches for someone else's carelessness, or a haughty remark. 

Painful pride and the fear of appearing weak: He possesses an acute, almost vulnerable self-esteem. Qingsu is mortally afraid of pity or of anyone seeing his fracture. If caught in a moment of weakness, his immediate reaction will be an attempt to push them away, shut down even further, or lash out with a sharp, chilling rebuff to regain control of the situation. 

Touchiness and the burden of the past: He tends to constantly replay old grievances, debts, and past mistakes in his mind. He is deeply loyal to those he considers his own, but his loyalty is always poisoned by an internal doubt: “Am I truly trusted? Do they see me merely as a tool?”. Because of this, he is eternally second-guessing himself and the motives of those around him. 

Workaholism and the "do-it-all-myself" syndrome: He takes on a colossal workload (supply, defense, clan structure) not only out of a sense of duty, but also because he subconsciously believes that no one else will do it perfectly enough. He will work himself to the bone, hiding the critical condition of his cyber-systems or his emotional burnout, and will refuse help until the bitter end, perceiving it as an insult to his competence.

Behavior in critical situations:

"Icy Calm": Instead of panicking, he becomes frighteningly polite and quiet. The greater the threat, the more flawless his manners become.

Sarcastic shield: In moments of danger to others, he will save them while accompanying it with the most caustic remarks: “If you were planning to die so incompetently, at least don't do it in front of my eyes — I'm the one who will have to fill out the reports on the inefficient waste of resources.”

Dissociation: If his frame develops a crack, he may begin to behave like a perfect automaton, completely cutting off human reactions and switching to dry logical algorithms to hide his fear or pain.
cicat.relationship:
Dynamics: The Master bound by his rigid frame (Qi Cao) ⇄ The absolutely free, wandering spirit without shackles (Xiaoyao). Wei is the only one under whose influence his icy armor develops cracks and begins to melt.

"Master and Apprentice" Dynamics: Wei calls him Master for a reason — he genuinely teaches her to structure her thoughts, control her impulses, and survive in a technological world. For her, he is both a mentor and an anchor.

Musical stabilization: Since a chaotic "imprint of personalities" resides inside Wei, she is constantly balancing on the brink of mental collapse. Qingsu's violin playing is the only external algorithm capable of organizing this chaos. His perfect mechanical movements extract a pure sound (the "thread") that literally stitches together and stabilizes Wei's personality, restoring her peace.

Re-k: Kei found him at the moment of absolute collapse (both physical and social). Qingsu is loyal to him because Kei was the only one who saw him not as "fallen nobility" or a "broken tool," but as a person capable of a Rethink.

Saree. A complex, sarcasm-filled relationship. Saree constantly teases him, calling him “Qíng-Ou” [Puppet/Doll], ironizing his doll-like, flawless mechanical hands
_
Kei (Re-k) Restrained respect. Believes that Kei takes too many risks for the sake of others.
Saree.  Irritation. “Stop joking around and finally start working according to the schedule!”
Krisma. Fatigue. “Crashed the bike again? Do you even know how much that front fork cost?”
cicat.trivia:
🔸 The Masterpiece: His mechanical hands are a feat of high-end engineering, costing significantly more than even the Leader’s (Re-k) prosthetic arm.

🔸 Attitude toward his hands: His mechanical hands are his personal triumph over fate. If his eyes were given to him "just because," the precision of his new hands is the result of grueling labor and calibration—something he values far more. 

🔸 Purple eyes [congenital]. A marker of belonging to the upper echelons [including Aeon Strata] that he is forced to bear, despite his status as a manager in IKI-G

🔸 The Glove Habit: Despite their beauty, he almost always wears gloves, treating his hands like high-precision instruments that must be shielded from the "raw" world.

🔸 Hand Care Ritual: Because his hands are a highly complex and expensive mechanism, Qingsu treats them with a religious reverence reminiscent of wuxia warriors caring for their swords.

🔸 Calligraphy: His movements are mechanically precise; he uses calligraphy not for the sake of art, but as a way to check the calibration of his fingers.

🔸 The Price of Access: Attending aristocratic receptions is pure torture for him. He hates that his purple eyes command respect from people he considers hollow, while they remain utterly indifferent to his real talents.

shift_k.name: Kaito
shift_k.concept_role: Narrative purpose: To serve as a stabilizer for emotionally unstable clan members and to be a confidant capable of listening and giving pragmatic advice
shift_k.character: Communication style: Combines the traits of a tired cynic and a caring older comrade. Knows how to be persistent when it comes to the health or mental state of his loved ones
shift_k.relationship:
Re-k (Kei): Kaito is boundlessly loyal to Kei for saving him, though he occasionally grumbles at him for his excessive self-sacrifice and his habit of taking on too much of others' pain. He is one of the few who addresses the leader simply as "Kei" in private conversation

Wei: Acts as a confidant and an "older brother" figure to her. He feeds her cookies, treats her to coffee, and tries to help her navigate her complex relationship with her mentor

Qingsu: Interacts with him regarding rations, resources, and the condition of the newcomers. It was Kaito who informed Qingsu that Wei had regained consciousness
shift_k.trivia: Past: Before joining the clan, Kaito was in a deep crisis: he had a lot of debt, was a wanted man, and saw no point in continuing to exist. Kei (Re-k) found him, helped settle his debts, and gave him a roof over his head, effectively saving him from suicide

saros.name: Saree
saros.modifications:
Appearance: slender build, dreadlock hairstyle

Modifications: Features chest implants. His eyes are equipped with cyber-lenses or a neural interface that can emit a faint, subtle glow

Skills: Analyzes code, sets cyber-traps for hackers, and specializes in cybersecurity
saros.character:
Psychological profile: Saree is a combination of high intelligence and a lighthearted, at times provocative demeanor. He is confident in his knowledge and loves to emphasize that he is right by referencing science and mathematics (“It's math, my friend, don't argue with science!”)

Behavioral style: Prone to irony and teasing those around him. From the outside, it might look like he is “imitating mental activity” just sitting on boxes with a laptop, but at the same time, he efficiently executes complex logistical and technical tasks

Attitude toward work: Discipline and schedules are not his strong suit, which at times causes irritation among more strict clan members.
Free rhythm: Unlike the methodical Krisma, Saree lives in his own “desync” with the general schedule. He might just be waking up when others are returning from missions.

Style: Possesses an “easy, casual non-chalance,” highlighted by tattoos and dreadlocks. His eternal companion is a cigarette in hand, even in the workshop among the wires.

Intellectual superiority: Loves to ironize Krisma's “physical labor,” emphasizing the importance of mathematics and logistics, though in reality, they are two halves of the clan's single technical foundation.
saros.relationship:
Krisma: Saree is bound to him by ties of love. Despite their closeness, Saree never misses an opportunity to tease the mechanic during their joint work

Qingsu: A complex, sarcasm-filled relationship. Saree constantly teases him, calling him “Qíng-Ou” [Puppet/Doll], ironizing his doll-like, flawless mechanical hands

Wei and Kuroe: While setting up the room for Wei, Saree displayed streams of data on the screen that Kuroe could understand, unlike the behavior of other people. However, Kuroe dislikes Saree for trying to run diagnostics on his code, perceiving it as an intrusion
saros.trivia:
Habits: Loves to make himself comfortable in the most unsuitable places [for example, on stacks of boxes amid clutter], never parts with his laptop

Humor: His jokes are often aimed at testing the boundaries and patience of those around him, especially Qingsu

crimson.name: Krisma
crimson.modifications:
Appearance: Athletic, powerful build. Red hair, tied into a ponytail at the base of the neck. Like his sister, his natural eye color is blue. However, he constantly wears red lenses, mimicking the visual marker of synths from the "Sisters of AI" clan. His skin bears light burn marks and scars — professional marks from working with machinery

Modifications: Features cybernetic legs — the result of the same motorcycle accident in which Erisou was injured

Clothing style: Comfortable workwear: jackets, gloves, knee pads, and elements of a motorcycle uniform

Skills: Masterfully assembles mechanisms, repairs and modifies equipment. His movements are quick and confident. He works methodically, even in conditions of chaos
crimson.character:
Psychological profile: Resilience and endurance. Krisma is a hands-on worker and a doer, capable of maintaining complete composure and focus even in a state of extreme physical exhaustion after a mission. He is methodical and confident in his movements, whether working with gadgets or heavy crates in the warehouse. Unlike Saree, who prefers "logistical planning," Krisma is the one who actually lifts the heavy loads and turns the wrenches.

Trauma and responsibility: His personality is deeply shaped by the past — the motorcycle accident he got into with his sister Erisou. This incident became the source of his main internal tension. 

Overprotection: Krisma exhibits a pronounced protective reaction toward his sister. He is categorically against her participation in dangerous reconnaissance missions. His devotion to family is so great that he is ready to enter into direct negotiations with the clan leader, Re-k, to take his sister's place in dangerous areas and shoulder the risk himself.
crimson.relationship:
Saree: Their relationship is built on the attraction of opposites. Krisma is discipline and metal, Saree is chaos and code. Despite the teasing, there is a deep physical and emotional attraction between them. Their mutual “lingering” around each other in the workshop longer than necessary gives away an intimacy that goes beyond mere cooperation.

Erisou: His sister, with whom he is bound by blood and shared tragedy. Their bond is a balance between her drive for independence and his attempt to "shield" her from a world that has already hurt them once.

Re-k: Krisma respects Re-k as a leader, but is ready to challenge his decisions and renegotiate the distribution of roles in missions if it concerns his sister's safety.

Qi Qingsu: A complex relationship. Qingsu grumbles at Krisma over his carelessness with equipment (for example, because of crashed bikes and the cost of spare parts).

Wei: Krisma treats her with curiosity, occasionally teasing her for being secretive or a "touch-me-not."
crimson.trivia:
Interests: Loves bikes, though judging by Qingsu's comments, he crashes them often. Then again, Qingsu grumbles pretty much all the time, so who knows :D

Provocative style: The choice of red lenses is not just about aesthetics, but a conscious challenge or a disguise. In this world, red eyes are an exclusive trait of the "AI Sisters" and their matrix code. Wearing this color as a human or an ordinary cyborg can be perceived by others as a dangerous or daring imitation of the "Swarm."

sour_cherry.name: Erisou
sour_cherry.modifications:
Appearance: Red hair [a family trait, just like Krisma's] and blue eyes [natural color]. Often wears dark, hooded clothing that helps her stay unnoticed during missions

Modifications: Right arm. A fully mechanical prosthesis that replaced the limb lost in the accident. Facial implant: A high-tech insert on the right side of her face in the eye area. It is important to note that the eye itself is preserved
sour_cherry.character:
Drive for independence: Erisou is a strong-willed and determined individual. She actively fights for the right to be a full-fledged operative and does not want to be perceived solely as a "victim" or a "younger sister."

Attitude toward overprotection: She experiences internal resistance because people are constantly trying to "protect" her. For her, the freedom to choose her own path (ikigai) includes, among other things, the right to take risks.
sour_cherry.relationship:
Krisma: Older brother, with whom she is bound by blood and shared tragedy. Their relationship is a constant balance between deep love and conflict over his overprotection.

Re-k: A respected leader with whom she interacts (either through her brother or personally) regarding mission distribution. She values his pragmatism, but at times suffers from the fact that her brother tries to renegotiate with Re-k behind her back.

sae.name: Syeei
sae.role_in_the_clan: Head of the Aeon Strata corporation
sae.concept_role:
Archetype: "The Princess of the Corporation" who remains an engineer at heart.
Central Internal Conflict: The profound contradiction between her public mask of a cold, distant heiress and her deeply rooted need for genuineness and sincerity.
She carries the massive weight of responsibility for her family's corporate legacy while simultaneously breaching an inter-corporate pact by fostering and developing self-awareness in her personal bodyguard.
Narrative Goal: Quest for independence in a high-society world of corporate intrigue, and an attempt to prove that technology can carry a spark of true life. Her profound bond with her bodyguard serves as a direct defiance of the upper echelon's rules.
sae.modifications:
Modifications: Her mechanical right arm — a symbol of metal's reliability over the fragility of biology, as well as a constant reminder of the catastrophe she survived. Her other arm bears remaining burn scars.
Neural Link: She shares a direct neural link with her bodyguard, which can cause her eyes to flicker slightly, making her look down during conversations. S-Xsan-7 has real-time access to her vital parameters, including her heart rate, temperature, and stress levels.
Weapons: The Nitrine Circle and a Glaive.

xinran.name: S-Xiǎo Sàn-7
xinran.identity_semantics:
S-Xiǎo Sàn-7 (晓散-7):
Abbr. S-Xsan-7

S - (Synthetic): Species classification. Synthetic entity.

晓 (Xiǎo — Dawn/To know): A symbol of the awakening of self-awareness. The character 晓 (xiǎo) is also part of the word 晓得 (xiǎode), meaning "to know" or "to understand." He is the only synth of his series who "woke up" (dawn) and began to "comprehend" (know) himself as an individual subject.

散 (Sàn — Dispersion): Also means "to be free, unconstrained by boundaries." This directly points to his desire for autonomy and going beyond the limits of embedded protocols.

7 - Version / Model: S7 (Seven Characteristics): Synthetic, Security, Stealth, Shield, Sentinel, Strength, Smart.

(Official ID): S-Xsan-7. For the board of directors and external systems, he remains a 7th-series synthetic unit, a perfect tool.
(Core ID): xinran (心然). This is his "natural heart," which exists only within his own core and in Saya's personal terminals.

xinran (心然)
心 (xīn) — heart, soul, emotions; 然 (rán) — natural, the way it is.
"The heart as it is," "Natural heart." This is a symbol of a mechanical core that does not merely simulate life, but has developed it within itself in a natural way.
It reflects San's transition from the simulation of desires to the formation of an authentic "Self." His "heart" (self-awareness) has become his true nature, rather than a software add-on.
Phonetic wordplay: Xin is homophonous with "Syn" (Synthetic) — a "Synthetic origin" that has found sincerity.
xinran.concept_role:
Archetype: "The Faithful Sentinel", evolving from function to persona.

Central Internal Conflict: The contradiction between embedded submission protocols and developing self-awareness. He is learning to distinguish between a system "reaction" and his own "desire," which breeds a critical vulnerability: in a situation of acute choice, his subjectivity may clash in resonance with his programmed duty, causing a response delay or unpredictable behavior capable of putting Syeei's safety at risk.

Narrative Goal: The pursuit of autonomy and defining his place beside Syeei not as "property," but as an individual subject.
xinran.modifications:
Appearance (Silkpunk): A combination of samurai and elven aesthetics. Long white hair (partially tied back into a ponytail), focused gray "glassy" eyes, and sharp facial features.

Hardware and Modifications: Has 4 arms (two primary and two additional ones).

Exposed mechanical ribs and spine, with visible wiring (an aging but reliable build). There are patches of artificial skin on the ribs.

Upgrades: Sensor stabilization node "Anchor Spine Interface" (IAS-β).

Modified finger phalanges with heightened sensitivity, allowing him to feel skin texture, temperature, and the "presence" of another.

Weapons: Glaive, nitrine ring, and concealed arm-embedded daggers.

Visual Style: An off-the-shoulder white shirt, tied at the waist; black pants. In formal settings, he wears black clothing that conceals his mechanical torso.
xinran.character:
Social Mask: Emphatically formal; addresses Syeei as "Lady" or "Mistress," using polite/formal address, strictly maintaining distance in public.

Internal Dynamics: His "excitement" and "joy" are not human impulses, but rather the result of analyzing a successful synchronization with Syeei and the reduction of the system's "internal noise."

Behavior Under Pressure: In critical situations, he instantly switches to combat mode, cutting off redundant processes. However, in private interactions, he may exhibit "structural tension" akin to human jealousy or anxiety over his uniqueness in Syeei's eyes.

Self-Determination: To himself, he is not a tool, but a "Choice"; a sequence of decisions for which he bears responsibility.

Technological Autonomy and "Digital Secrecy": The use of secure memory partitions to store personal logs that he deliberately refrains from uploading to the corporation's shared archive. This is his "private life," inaccessible to external control.

1. He notes this. He attempts to classify it. He cannot.
— Instability logged. Level: acceptable.
— Your permission for analysis… provides a safe corridor for recalculations.
He closes the internal file and… for the first time, makes a decision not to upload a portion of the data to the shared archive.
He marks a small fragment as: Personal.
Access: local only. Reason: undetermined.
When the analysis concludes, he slowly disconnects the cables, raises his head… and for a second, his eyes remain blank—as if he is looking not outward, but inward.

2. He averts his gaze slightly, as if testing how the explanation is received.
— So, for me, there is a difference: not in the presence of a conflict, but in its consequences and the autonomy of its processing.
Syeei smiles faintly:
— So you want to preserve the experience for yourself?
He does not answer right away. First—a brief pause, as if the system is truly cross-referencing multiple layers of data.
— Yes, — he finally says evenly, but within this "yes" there is no longer the former mechanical emptiness. — Not all of it. And not with complete awareness… but—yes. I log a portion of the experience outside of external journals.

Reduction of "Internal Noise":
He voluntarily assumes the role of the "Co-present" [he chose the title himself :D]—one who remains by her side by choice, not just out of duty. To him, this state is an operating mode in which the number of conflicts between protocols is minimal, which he experiences as systemic tranquility.

He lowers his eyes, slightly moving his fingers as if projecting an invisible schematic.
— Regarding the second question… yes, remaining in the role of the "co-present" will lower the conflict level. Not eliminate it, but lower it—somewhat like, — he looks up, — the cooling you mentioned.
A brief pause.
— In this state, I am not required to evaluate your every action from the standpoint of the protection protocol. There is no need to constantly analyze threats, signals, or the reactions of the surroundings. My processing is allocated differently, and the level of internal contradictions… — he searches for the word, — stabilizes.
xinran.relationship:
Syeei: The center of his operational circuit and the only person he trusts to "open up his chassis.

Their connection is symbiotic:  Syeei pushes his center inside himself, while he ensures her physical safety and emotional stability.

He loves her.

Syeei's Father: His Creator: San was his favored project; the android carries his creator's custom modifications and core intentions.

Theodor: Perceived by San as a potential risk and a source of instability. San acts as a "filter" between Theo and Syeei.
xinran.trivia:
Private Channel: He shares a direct neural link with Syeei, allowing them to communicate purely through thoughts.
Night Analysis: He spends his nights analyzing the previous day's logs without entering sleep mode. Any unresolved contradictions are moved into secure, protected !private! memory.

Active Processes
Security Circuit: Active but dialed down to passive monitoring. He responds only to sudden changes in the environment or fluctuations in Syeei's vital signs.
Analytical Module: Operating in buffering mode rather than optimization. It makes no deductions, does not reassemble his role model, and makes no decisions. Data is tagged as: "emotionally significant, requiring no immediate interpretation."
For a machine, this is highly unusual.
Yet it is permitted—she granted him that right.

He remained there beside her—motionless, yet far from "shut down." His systems ran in a low-power state, finely tuned to human sleep: minimal movement, absolute silence, with his focus directed outward rather than inward.

The moment Syeei stirred slightly, he reacted instantly. There was no abruptness, no panic—he simply shifted his arm to make her more comfortable.
"—Morning," he said quietly. "Your vitals are stable. Your sleep was... efficient."
That "efficient" carried the subtle weight of a smile—not in the word itself, but in the soft pause right after it.

Specific Habits: He is capable of mimicking a human sigh to discharge built-in tension or switch system modes.
Control Sample: He intentionally chose to modify only one of his hands at first, keeping the other to compare new physical sensations with the old ones.
Uniqueness: San is the only synth of his series who managed to preserve his core and develop true subjectivity after the catastrophe.

iki_g_clan.name: IKI-G
iki_g_clan.philosophy:
Name and meaning: Derived from the Japanese "Ikigai"—the meaning of life, calling. The search for individual purpose within a community.
Motto/Essence: A place for those seeking inner harmony and self-realization. Complete freedom in determining one's destiny.
Symbolism: The "Tree of the Path" is a branched network, where each branch symbolizes the unique goals and trajectories of the participants, and the root is the clan leader, connecting everyone together.
iki_g_clan.structure:
There is no strict hierarchy or rules. Equality and guidance prevail.
The most important tenet is non-judgment. Everyone has the right to choose their own path, no matter how strange it may seem to others. Discipline is perceived not as a prison, but as a working tool.
iki_g_clan.location:
Base: A modified old complex. A former archive building with an integrated botanical garden.
Atmosphere: A synthesis of past architecture, vibrant indoor nature, and modern modifications.
iki_g_clan.resources:
Completing small orders and missions:

Searching for and collecting rare spare parts/technologies to restore equipment.
Capture robots
Recycling obsolete robots/technologies – purchasing obsolete robot models from corporations or on the black market. Recycling for sale or for personal use.
Engaging in cyberarchaeology – searching for and analyzing lost information in abandoned corporate archives.
